item.title: Pimco Seeks to Sell Parts of $14 Billion Oracle Data Center Debt
item.description: Pacific Investment Management Co. is looking to sell a portion of the $14 billion of debt financing it’s providing for a massive Oracle Corp. data center in Michigan, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Singapore Government Offices Told to Rein in Air-Conditioning
item.description: Government facilities in Singapore will take steps to reduce electricity consumption to boost the nation’s energy resilience as the conflict in the Middle East tightens global energy supplies.

item.title: Anthropic Fails for Now to Halt US Label as a Supply-Chain Risk
item.description: A federal appeals court declined for now Anthropic PBC’s request to pause a declaration by the Pentagon that the artificial intelligence company poses a risk to the US supply chain, even as plans for a broader government ban on its technology remain blocked by a California judge.

item.title: UK Homebuyer Demand Slumps as Borrowing Costs Spiral, RICS Says
item.description: The number of Britons looking to buy a home nosedived in March after the mortgage market was rocked by conflict in the Middle East, according to the Royal Institution of Chartered Surveyors.

item.title: China Taps Telecom Executive as Senior Colonel for Reserve Force
item.description: China’s military appointed an executive from a state telecommunications company as a senior colonel in its reserve force, highlighting Beijing’s continued efforts to integrate civilian technology with its armed forces.

item.title: North Korea Conducted Test of Electromagnetic Weapon System, KCNA Says
item.description: North Korea conducted a slew of new weapons tests, the state run Korean Central News Agency said, including one involving an electromagnetic weapon system and a carbon fiber bomb.

item.title: Lawmaker Urges SEC to Probe ‘Suspicious’ Trading Amid Iran War
item.description: Wall Street’s top regulators should investigate unusual trading activity in oil and equity futures markets shortly before President Donald Trump postponed military strikes against Iran last month, a lawmaker said Wednesday.

item.title: Gold Steadies as Traders Weigh Fragile Ceasefire in Iran War
item.description: Gold steadied after a two-day gain as traders weighed the prospect for a diplomatic resolution to the Iran war, even as sporadic fighting threatened to derail a fragile ceasefire.

item.title: US Futures Waver as Iran Says Ceasefire Violated: Markets Wrap
item.description: US equity-index futures wavered and oil pared some of Wednesday’s losses, underscoring fragility in markets after Tehran said several clauses of its ceasefire agreement with Washington had been violated.

item.title: UBS-Led Group Funds Echo Global Debt as Investor Talks Pause
item.description: A lender group led by UBS Group AG has financed the tie-up of two logistics firms after pausing early talks to offload the debt to investors amid market volatility.

item.title: Oil Rises After Biggest Drop Since 2020 as Hormuz Stays Blocked
item.description: Oil rebounded after its biggest one-day drop since April 2020, as the Strait of Hormuz remained largely blocked and Israeli attacks on Lebanon threatened to derail the fragile ceasefire in the Middle East.

item.title: US Mulls Lifting Venezuela Central Bank Sanctions to Aid Economy
item.description: The US is considering lifting sanctions on Venezuela’s central bank to facilitate the flow of billions of dollars into the country’s battered economy, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Colombia Downgraded by S&P Ratings Amid Growing Fiscal Concerns
item.description: Colombia’s credit score was downgraded by S&P Global Ratings for the second time in less than a year on concerns over a persistently large fiscal deficit and high debt burden.

item.title: BOJ Is Likely to Raise Interest Rate in April, Ex-Official Says
item.description: The Bank of Japan will probably increase its benchmark rate this month to avoid falling behind on controlling inflation, according to a former executive director at the central bank.

item.title: DOJ Top Antitrust Litigators Exit After Ticketmaster Settlement
item.description: The US Justice Department’s top antitrust litigator and three senior trial attorneys that led cases against Live Nation Entertainment Inc., Apple Inc. and Alphabet Inc.’s Google are leaving the agency, according to people familiar with their plans.

item.title: Novo-Backed Respiratory Drugmaker Avalyn Pharma Files for US IPO
item.description: Avalyn Pharma Inc., a biopharma company developing inhaled treatments for respiratory diseases, filed for a US initial public offering.

item.title: Aritzia Shares Soar Most in Nearly a Year on Strong Spring Collection Sales
item.description: Clothing retailer Aritzia Inc.’s shares rallied the most in nearly a year and led the Canadian equity benchmark higher on Wednesday, as data showed sales got a big boost from the latest spring collection and prospects for an end to the Iran war broadly spurred risk sentiment.

item.title: BTG Deal to Buy Digimais Said to Get Insurance Fund Support
item.description: Banco BTG Pactual SA’s deal to buy Banco Digimais SA, a struggling Brazilian lender in need of capital, is getting support from the FGC, the nation’s deposit insurance fund, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Time Is on Canada’s Side in USMCA Talks, Former Trade Chief Says
item.description: Pressure on the US economy is building and Canada can likely get the best outcome in trade negotiations by waiting, according to the former Canadian negotiator who helped draft a new deal during President Donald Trump’s first term.

item.title: Watch the Price of Russian Oil
item.description: Another flavor of crude.

item.title: Muni Bonds Rally by Most in a Year as Markets Welcome Ceasefire
item.description: State and local government bonds surged by the most in a year after the ceasefire to the US war against Iran unleashed relief rallies across global financial markets.

item.title: Banks Selling $3 Billion Debt for Meta’s Prometheus Data Center
item.description: A group of banks including Natixis SA, Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group Inc. and Societe Generale SA have started selling $3 billion of loans for a data center in Ohio backed by Meta Platforms Inc., the latest deal supporting the buildout of artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: Easing Mideast Tensions Could Boost Private Equity Dealmaking, Baratta Says
item.description: An easing of hostilities in the Middle East may create conditions that bolster private equity dealmaking through the rest of the year, according to Blackstone Inc.’s Joe Baratta.

item.title: Canadian Crude Fetches Biggest Gulf Coast Premium in Two Years
item.description: It is the most lucrative time in more than two years to ship Canadian crude to the US Gulf Coast, thanks to the effective closing of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Shippers Rush for Hormuz Insurance After Truce Deal, Broker Says
item.description: Shipowners are placing “huge volume requests” for insurance cover as they look to transit the Strait of Hormuz following the US-Iran ceasefire deal, according to broker McGill and Partners.

item.title: France to Keep 5% 2026 Deficit Goal for Now, Budget Chief Says
item.description: France’s budget minister said it’s too soon to set a more ambitious deficit reduction target despite a sharper-than-expected narrowing of the gap last year.

item.title: Fed Minutes Show Officials Saw Two-Sided Risks From Iran War
item.description: A growing number of Federal Reserve officials worried the Iran war could further stoke inflation and wanted to make clear following their March meeting that the central bank may have to consider raising interest rates.

item.title: ICE’s Phone Spyware Plans Face Questions From US Lawmaker
item.description: The agency has a deal with Israeli vendor Paragon.

item.title: Short Sellers Squeezed Hard as Avis Budget Shares Rocket 150%
item.description: A highly shorted stock. A shrinking pool of shares available to trade. A price that keeps climbing.

item.title: Russia Ramps Up Spending Ahead of Windfall From Middle East War
item.description: Russia stepped up spending in March in anticipation of a surge in revenue from the jump in oil prices caused by the war in the Middle East.
